title: What to Know Before Buying a Home in Clermont, Florida
excerpt: Clermont has quickly become one of Central Florida's most sought-after communities. Before you start touring homes, here's what every buyer should understand about this market.
body:
Clermont sits on a chain of lakes in Lake County, just west of Orlando, and it has grown dramatically over the past decade. New construction, top-rated schools, and easy access to major highways have made it a top destination for families, retirees, and investors alike. If you are considering buying here, understanding the local market dynamics is essential before you make an offer.

One of the most important things to know is that Clermont's inventory moves fast. Well-priced homes in desirable neighborhoods often receive multiple offers within days of listing. This means buyers need to be fully pre-approved — not just pre-qualified — before they start seriously shopping. Michele works closely with trusted local lenders who can get you to the finish line quickly and cleanly.

The area also has a wide range of property types, from new-build communities with HOA amenities to older lakefront homes with large lots and no restrictions. Each comes with different cost considerations. New construction can seem straightforward, but the builder's contract is written to protect the builder — having an experienced agent in your corner is critical to protecting your interests and negotiating upgrades and closing costs.

Finally, don't overlook the opportunity in pre-foreclosures and distressed properties in the area. Michele specializes in exactly these kinds of transactions and has helped numerous buyers secure homes significantly below market value. If you are open to a property that needs work, this can be one of the smartest financial moves you make.
author: Michele Rueff

title: 5 Signs It's Time to Sell Your Home
excerpt: Timing the sale of your home is one of the most consequential decisions you can make. These five signals suggest the moment may be right.
body:
Deciding when to sell is rarely straightforward. Life circumstances, market conditions, and your financial goals all play a role. But there are a handful of reliable signals that, when they appear together, suggest the timing is in your favor.

The first and most obvious sign is that your neighborhood is appreciating quickly. If comparable homes nearby are selling above asking price and sitting on the market for just a few days, you are in a seller's market — and that leverage belongs to you. Central Florida, and Clermont in particular, has seen sustained appreciation over recent years, making this a favorable environment for sellers.

The second sign is that your home no longer fits your life. Whether your family has grown, your kids have moved out, you are relocating for work, or you simply want a change of scenery, life transitions are a practical and valid reason to sell. Holding onto a home that no longer serves you while the market is strong is leaving money on the table.

Third, if your equity has grown significantly, selling may unlock capital you can use toward your next chapter — a larger home, an investment property, or financial flexibility. Michele can walk you through a free home valuation to show you exactly where you stand.

Fourth, rising maintenance costs on an aging property can signal that it makes more financial sense to sell than to keep pouring money into repairs. And fifth, if interest rates are favorable for buyers in the market, more qualified buyers are shopping — which means more competition for your listing and a higher probability of strong offers.
author: Michele Rueff

title: Understanding Pre-Foreclosures: A Buyer's Guide to Below-Market Deals
excerpt: Pre-foreclosure properties can offer significant savings, but they come with unique risks and timelines. Here's how to navigate them successfully.
body:
A pre-foreclosure occurs when a homeowner has fallen behind on mortgage payments and the lender has issued a notice of default, but the property has not yet been auctioned. This window — which can last several months — creates an opportunity for buyers to purchase the home directly from the owner, often below market value.

For buyers willing to do their homework, pre-foreclosures can be exceptional deals. The seller is typically motivated and the transaction can be handled as a standard purchase rather than a foreclosure auction, which means you can conduct a full inspection, secure traditional financing, and negotiate a clean title transfer. This is a significant advantage over buying at auction, where you are often purchasing sight-unseen and may inherit liens or title issues.

However, these deals require speed, patience, and expertise in equal measure. The homeowner is in a stressful situation and negotiations must be handled with sensitivity. You will also need to move quickly — the foreclosure timeline does not pause for indecisive buyers. Having an agent who understands this process inside and out is not optional; it is essential.

Michele has personally bought and navigated dozens of pre-foreclosure transactions throughout Central Florida. She knows how to identify these opportunities before they hit the public market, how to structure offers that protect buyers while moving the deal forward efficiently, and how to work through the title and lender complications that often arise. If you are interested in exploring pre-foreclosures as part of your buying strategy, reach out for a conversation.
author: Michele Rueff
